Functions for managing Bluetooth reconnection, including pause, resume, and reset operations.

# Purpose
This code is a C source file that defines a set of functions related to Bluetooth driver reconnection management. The functions [`bt_driver_reconnect_pause`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_pause>) and [`bt_driver_reconnect_resume`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_resume>) are likely intended to control the pausing and resuming of reconnection attempts. The function [`bt_driver_reconnect_try_now`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_try_now>) takes a boolean parameter `ignore_paused`, which suggests it attempts an immediate reconnection, potentially bypassing any paused state. The [`bt_driver_reconnect_reset_interval`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_reset_interval>) function likely resets the reconnection interval to its initial state. Lastly, [`bt_driver_reconnect_notify_platform_bitfield`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_notify_platform_bitfield>) accepts a `uint32_t` parameter `platform_bitfield`, but the function body indicates that the parameter is not currently used.

- `stdbool.h`
